title: Assertions should be complete
description: Assertions should be completeAnalyze your codeadaptability-testedmaintainabilityCode SmelltestsWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThis rule addresses the issue of incomplete assertions that can occur when using certain test frameworks. Incomplete assertions can lead to tests that do not effectively verify anything. The rule enforces the use of complete assertions in specific cases, namely:Fluent Assertions:Should()is not followed by an assertion invocation.string actual = "Using Fluent Assertions"; actual.Should(); // NoncompliantNFluent:Check.That()is not followed by an assertion invocation.string actual = "Using NFluent"; Check.That(actual); // NoncompliantNSubstitute:Received()is not followed by an invocation.command.Received(); // NoncompliantIn such cases, what is intended
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
string actual = "Using Fluent Assertions";
actual.Should(); // Noncompliant - incomplete assertion

string actual = "Using NFluent";
Check.That(actual); // Noncompliant - incomplete assertion
compliant:
string actual = "Using Fluent Assertions";
actual.Should().Be("expected"); // Compliant - complete assertion

string actual = "Using NFluent";
Check.That(actual).IsEqualTo("expected"); // Compliant - complete assertion

checks.message: Complete the assertion by adding the verification method (e.g., .Be(), .Execute()).
